title: Device Object
description: Package variant within a device set; combines a symbol configuration with a specific package.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: gates
properties.description: Collection of gates in this device.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: library
properties.description: Name of the library where this device is defined.

properties.name: libraryVersion
properties.description: Version number of the source library.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: package
properties.description: Package associated with this device (can be null).

properties.name: technologies
properties.description: Collection of technologies for this device.

related_samples.name: Electronics Bill of Materials Sample
related_samples.description: Prints a bill of materials to the Text Commands window: lines that share the same value, footprint, MPN, and manufacturer are merged—Part lists all designators (sorted), Qty is the count. Indented Value, Footprint, MPN, and Manufacturer lines follow. Empty value, MPN, or manufacturer shows (missing). Omits parts without a package, without a linked 3D footprint name, or not populated for the default assembly variant.
